Software Engineering Intern — AI & Data Pipelines

Company: EXAai Demo Labs
Location: Remote (US/EU time zones)
Experience: Intern / new grad (0–1 years; coursework and personal projects welcome)

About the role
We are looking for a motivated intern to support our AI-powered hiring and knowledge tools team. You will learn how document ingestion, embeddings, vector search, and LLM workflows fit together in production Python services—not just notebooks. Mentorship is provided; curiosity and follow-through matter more than years of experience.

Key responsibilities
- Assist with building and testing RAG pipeline components (ingest, chunk, embed, retrieve).
- Write and maintain small Python scripts and FastAPI endpoints with mentor feedback.
- Help integrate LLM APIs (Gemini or similar) with structured JSON outputs for screening workflows.
- Run experiments on chunking, retrieval quality, and basic evaluation metrics.
- Document findings, write unit tests, and participate in code reviews.
- Learn secure handling of PII, allowlisted crawlers, and prompt-injection basics.

Requirements:
- Must have: Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in CS, engineering, or related field
- Must have: Solid Python fundamentals (functions, classes, async basics, pip/venv)
- Must have: Familiarity with REST APIs and HTTP (GET/POST, JSON request/response)
- Required: At least one completed project using Python (coursework, hackathon, or GitHub)
- Required: Basic Git workflow (clone, branch, commit, pull request)
- Required: Comfort reading documentation and debugging with logs or a debugger
- Required: Clear written communication in English

Nice to have:
- Preferred: Exposure to FastAPI, Flask, or Django
- Preferred: Introductory ML or NLP coursework (embeddings, transformers at a high level)
- Preferred: Personal projects with LLMs, LangChain, or similar tutorials
- Bonus: SQL or PostgreSQL basics
- Bonus: Docker or GitHub Actions from a class or side project
- Bonus: Interest in retrieval-augmented generation or agent tool-calling patterns
